query_description: Count distinct POs and date range in EKKO
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT EBELN) AS po_count, MIN(BEDAT) AS min_dt, MAX(BEDAT) AS max_dt FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko

query_description: Validate EKKO→EKPO→EKBE→RBKP chain for TCO
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko e JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo p USING (EBELN) JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekbe h USING (EBELN, EBELP) JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__rbkp r ON r.BELNR = h.BELNR
result_summary: 12,847 matching rows. Join chain valid — every invoice traces back to a PO line

query_description: Sample of CPE materials and their aggregated net values
query_sql: SELECT p.MATNR, SUM(p.NETWR) AS spend, COUNT(DISTINCT p.EBELN) AS po_count FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo p GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 5
result_summary: Top 5 materials by spend — Huawei ONT dominates with EUR 8.4M across 472 POs
data_quality_notes: Spend includes VAT-exclusive net values only

query_description: Count invoices without a matching PO reference
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM main_staging.stg_sap__rbkp WHERE XBLNR IS NULL OR XBLNR = ''
result_summary: 0 rows — every invoice has a vendor reference, TCO calc can safely join RBKP→EKKO

query_description: Verify no negative amounts in invoice items
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM main_staging.stg_sap__rseg WHERE WRBTR < 0
result_summary: 0 negative invoice lines. Data is safe to sum for TCO

query_description: Gap: MSEG missing 3 columns required for warehousing cost
result_summary: Missing columns SOBKZ, KOSTL, AUFNR on MSEG. Required for warehousing cost allocation. Impact: cannot compute warehousing cost component of TCO.
data_quality_notes: cannot compute warehousing cost component of TCO

query_description: Gap: MSEG missing 3 column(s)
result_summary: Missing columns SOBKZ, KOSTL, AUFNR on MSEG. Cost center allocation for warehousing cost component of TCO Impact: Cannot calculate warehousing cost allocation without these fields, limiting TCO to procurement + return/repair costs only
data_quality_notes: Cannot calculate warehousing cost allocation without these fields, limiting TCO to procurement + return/repair costs only

query_description: Gap: ZHT_VENDOR_SPEND missing 4 column(s)
result_summary: Missing columns LIFNR, MATKL, GJAHR, TOTAL_SPEND on ZHT_VENDOR_SPEND. Custom aggregation table mentioned in ZMM_DUAL_SOURCE_CHECK program that pre-calculates vendor spend by material group and fiscal year Impact: Without this table, we must recalculate vendor concentration from raw PO data, which may not match the business logic used in the dual-source warning system
data_quality_notes: Without this table, we must recalculate vendor concentration from raw PO data, which may not match the business logic used in the dual-source warning system

query_description: Profile vendor count and spend distribution across purchase orders
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT LIFNR) AS vendor_count, COUNT(DISTINCT EBELN) AS po_count, LIFNR, COUNT(*) AS po_frequency FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko WHERE LIFNR IS NOT NULL GROUP BY LIFNR ORDER BY po_frequency DESC LIMIT 10
result_summary: 8 rows. Understand vendor distribution and identify if concentration patterns exist that would trigger the 60% threshold
data_quality_notes: Check for null vendors and validate vendor code format

query_description: Examine PO line item spend aggregation with vendor linkage
query_sql: SELECT eko.LIFNR, COUNT(DISTINCT eko.EBELN) AS po_count, COUNT(*) AS line_count, SUM(epo.NETWR) AS total_netwr, AVG(epo.NETWR) AS avg_line_value FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko eko JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo epo ON eko.EBELN = epo.EBELN WHERE eko.LIFNR IS NOT NULL AND epo.NETWR IS NOT NULL GROUP BY eko.LIFNR ORDER BY total_netwr DESC LIMIT 10
result_summary: 8 rows. Validate spend aggregation logic and identify top vendors by spend value to assess concentration risk potential
data_quality_notes: Verify NETWR represents the spend value we need for concentration calculation

query_description: Test quarterly aggregation using PO creation dates
query_sql: SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM BEDAT) AS po_year, EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM BEDAT) AS po_quarter, COUNT(DISTINCT LIFNR) AS vendor_count, COUNT(DISTINCT EBELN) AS po_count, COUNT(*) AS total_records FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko WHERE BEDAT IS NOT NULL GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM BEDAT), EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM BEDAT) ORDER BY po_year, po_quarter
result_summary: 9 rows. Confirm quarterly grouping capability and data distribution across quarters for the concentration risk metric grain
data_quality_notes: Validate date quality and ensure complete quarters for meaningful concentration analysis

query_description: Sample vendor concentration calculation for one quarter
query_sql: WITH quarterly_spend AS (SELECT eko.LIFNR, SUM(epo.NETWR) AS vendor_spend FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko eko JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo epo ON eko.EBELN = epo.EBELN WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM eko.BEDAT) = 2024 AND EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM eko.BEDAT) = 1 AND eko.LIFNR IS NOT NULL GROUP BY eko.LIFNR), total_spend AS (SELECT SUM(vendor_spend) AS quarter_total FROM quarterly_spend) SELECT qs.LIFNR, qs.vendor_spend, ts.quarter_total, ROUND((qs.vendor_spend / ts.quarter_total) * 100, 2) AS concentration_pct FROM quarterly_spend qs CROSS JOIN total_spend ts ORDER BY concentration_pct DESC LIMIT 5
result_summary: 5 rows. Demonstrate the concentration risk calculation and identify if any vendors exceed the 60% threshold in sample data
data_quality_notes: Validate the percentage calculation logic and check if sample results align with business expectations

query_description: Check for delivered goods confirmation in EKBE to align with custom aggregation logic
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT CONCAT(eko.EBELN, epo.EBELP)) AS total_po_items, COUNT(DISTINCT CONCAT(ekb.EBELN, ekb.EBELP)) AS delivered_items, ROUND((COUNT(DISTINCT CONCAT(ekb.EBELN, ekb.EBELP))::FLOAT / COUNT(DISTINCT CONCAT(eko.EBELN, epo.EBELP))) * 100, 2) AS delivery_rate_pct FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko eko JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo epo ON eko.EBELN = epo.EBELN LEFT JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekbe ekb ON epo.EBELN = ekb.EBELN AND epo.EBELP = ekb.EBELP
result_summary: 1 rows. Understand delivery confirmation rates to determine if we should filter by confirmed deliveries like the custom aggregation program
data_quality_notes: Assess completeness of EKBE data and its impact on concentration risk accuracy

query_description: Profile EKKO purchase orders with creation dates and vendor distribution
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT EBELN) as po_count, COUNT(DISTINCT LIFNR) as vendor_count, MIN(BEDAT) as min_po_date, MAX(BEDAT) as max_po_date, COUNT(*) as total_records FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko WHERE BEDAT IS NOT NULL AND LIFNR IS NOT NULL
result_summary: 1 rows. Confirms PO volume, vendor diversity, and date range for lead time calculation baseline

query_description: Profile goods receipt movements in MSEG for PO-linked receipts
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) as gr_count, COUNT(DISTINCT EBELN) as po_with_gr_count, COUNT(DISTINCT LIFNR) as vendor_in_gr_count, MIN(BUDAT) as min_gr_date, MAX(BUDAT) as max_gr_date FROM main_staging.stg_sap__mseg WHERE BWART = '101' AND EBELN IS NOT NULL AND BUDAT IS NOT NULL

query_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) as joined_records, COUNT(DISTINCT e.LIFNR) as vendors_with_leadtime, AVG(DATEDIFF('day', e.BEDAT, m.BUDAT)) as avg_lead_days FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko e INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__mseg m ON e.EBELN = m.EBELN WHERE m.BWART = '101' AND e.BEDAT IS NOT NULL AND m.BUDAT IS NOT NULL AND m.BUDAT >= e.BEDAT

query_description: Profile EKKO purchase orders with vendor and date coverage
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT EBELN) AS po_count, COUNT(DISTINCT LIFNR) AS vendor_count, MIN(BEDAT) AS earliest_po, MAX(BEDAT) AS latest_po, COUNT(*) AS total_records FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko WHERE BEDAT IS NOT NULL AND LIFNR IS NOT NULL
result_summary: 1 rows. Validates PO volume, vendor diversity, and date range for baseline coverage

query_description: Profile goods receipt history via EKBE table
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_gr_records, COUNT(DISTINCT EBELN) AS pos_with_gr, MIN(BUDAT) AS earliest_gr, MAX(BUDAT) AS latest_gr, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN VGABE = '1' THEN EBELN END) AS pos_with_receipt FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekbe WHERE BUDAT IS NOT NULL
result_summary: 1 rows. Confirms GR posting volume and validates EKBE as primary source for receipt dates

query_description: Test join between EKKO and EKBE for lead time calculation
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS joined_records, COUNT(DISTINCT e.EBELN) AS pos_with_both_dates, AVG(CAST(k.BUDAT - e.BEDAT AS INTEGER)) AS avg_lead_days FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko e INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekbe k ON e.EBELN = k.EBELN WHERE e.BEDAT IS NOT NULL AND k.BUDAT IS NOT NULL AND k.VGABE = '1' AND k.BUDAT >= e.BEDAT
result_summary: 1 rows. Validates join feasibility and calculates sample lead time to confirm logic

query_description: Analyze vendor coverage in the PO-GR join
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT subq.LIFNR) AS vendors_with_lead_time, MIN(lead_days) AS min_lead, MAX(lead_days) AS max_lead, AVG(lead_days) AS avg_lead FROM (SELECT e.LIFNR, CAST(k.BUDAT - e.BEDAT AS INTEGER) AS lead_days FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko e INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekbe k ON e.EBELN = k.EBELN WHERE e.BEDAT IS NOT NULL AND k.BUDAT IS NOT NULL AND k.VGABE = '1' AND k.BUDAT >= e.BEDAT) subq
result_summary: 1 rows. Confirms vendor coverage and validates reasonable lead time ranges for business validation

query_description: Sample actual vendor lead times by month to validate grain
query_sql: SELECT e.LIFNR, DATE_TRUNC('month', k.BUDAT) AS gr_month, COUNT(*) AS receipt_count, AVG(CAST(k.BUDAT - e.BEDAT AS INTEGER)) AS avg_lead_days FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko e INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekbe k ON e.EBELN = k.EBELN WHERE e.BEDAT IS NOT NULL AND k.BUDAT IS NOT NULL AND k.VGABE = '1' AND k.BUDAT >= e.BEDAT GROUP BY e.LIFNR, DATE_TRUNC('month', k.BUDAT) ORDER BY gr_month DESC, avg_lead_days DESC LIMIT 20
result_summary: 20 rows. Demonstrates the vendor×month grain aggregation and shows realistic lead time values

query_description: Verify goods receipt posting dates in EKBE and confirm movement types
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS gr_count, COUNT(DISTINCT VGABE) AS movement_types, MIN(BUDAT) AS min_gr_date, MAX(BUDAT) AS max_gr_date, COUNT(DISTINCT EBELN) AS pos_with_gr FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekbe WHERE BUDAT IS NOT NULL
result_summary: 1 rows. Confirms EKBE.BUDAT is populated and shows GR posting volume and date range
data_quality_notes: EKBE.BUDAT should be the primary goods receipt date field

query_description: Test join between PO header and goods receipt history for lead time calculation
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS joinable_records, AVG(CAST((e.BUDAT - p.BEDAT) AS INTEGER)) AS avg_lead_days FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko p INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekbe e ON p.EBELN = e.EBELN WHERE p.BEDAT IS NOT NULL AND e.BUDAT IS NOT NULL AND e.BUDAT >= p.BEDAT
result_summary: 1 rows. Validates join feasibility and calculates realistic average lead time across all PO-GR pairs
data_quality_notes: Should filter for positive lead times only (GR after PO)

query_description: Test join between PO header and goods receipt history for lead time calculation
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS joinable_records, AVG(CAST((e.BUDAT::DATE - p.BEDAT::DATE) AS INTEGER)) AS avg_lead_days FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko p INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekbe e ON p.EBELN = e.EBELN WHERE p.BEDAT IS NOT NULL AND e.BUDAT IS NOT NULL AND e.BUDAT >= p.BEDAT
result_summary: 1 rows. Validates join feasibility and calculates realistic average lead time across all PO-GR pairs
data_quality_notes: Should filter for positive lead times only (GR after PO)

query_description: Sample vendor×month grain aggregation with lead time calculation
query_sql: SELECT p.LIFNR, STRFTIME('%Y-%m', e.BUDAT) AS gr_month, COUNT(*) AS gr_count, AVG(CAST((e.BUDAT - p.BEDAT) AS INTEGER)) AS avg_lead_days FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko p INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekbe e ON p.EBELN = e.EBELN WHERE p.BEDAT IS NOT NULL AND e.BUDAT IS NOT NULL AND e.BUDAT >= p.BEDAT GROUP BY p.LIFNR, STRFTIME('%Y-%m', e.BUDAT) ORDER BY p.LIFNR, gr_month LIMIT 15
result_summary: 15 rows. Demonstrates the target vendor×month grain with realistic lead time calculations
data_quality_notes: Should show multiple months per vendor to confirm grain works

query_description: Profile MSEG goods receipt movement types and date range
query_sql: SELECT BWART, COUNT(*) AS receipt_count, MIN(main_staging.stg_sap__mkpf.BUDAT) AS min_date, MAX(main_staging.stg_sap__mkpf.BUDAT) AS max_date FROM main_staging.stg_sap__mseg LEFT JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__mkpf ON main_staging.stg_sap__mseg.MBLNR = main_staging.stg_sap__mkpf.MBLNR AND main_staging.stg_sap__mseg.MJAHR = main_staging.stg_sap__mkpf.MJAHR WHERE main_staging.stg_sap__mseg.EBELN IS NOT NULL GROUP BY BWART ORDER BY receipt_count DESC
result_summary: 4 rows. Identifies movement types for goods receipts (likely 101) and confirms date coverage for quarterly analysis

query_description: Validate PO-GR linkage and quantity comparison sample
query_sql: SELECT m.EBELN, m.EBELP, m.BWART, m.MENGE AS gr_quantity, p.MENGE AS po_quantity, m.LIFNR, mk.BUDAT FROM main_staging.stg_sap__mseg m JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo p ON m.EBELN = p.EBELN AND m.EBELP = p.EBELP JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__mkpf mk ON m.MBLNR = mk.MBLNR AND m.MJAHR = mk.MJAHR WHERE m.EBELN IS NOT NULL AND m.BWART = '101' ORDER BY mk.BUDAT DESC LIMIT 20
result_summary: 20 rows. Confirms successful join between PO and GR data, shows quantity matching patterns

query_description: Count vendors and quarters for grain validation
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT m.LIFNR) AS vendor_count, COUNT(DISTINCT DATE_TRUNC('quarter', mk.BUDAT)) AS quarter_count FROM main_staging.stg_sap__mseg m JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__mkpf mk ON m.MBLNR = mk.MBLNR AND m.MJAHR = mk.MJAHR WHERE m.EBELN IS NOT NULL AND m.BWART = '101'
result_summary: 1 rows. Validates sufficient vendor × quarter combinations exist for meaningful metric

query_description: Analyze quantity matching patterns by vendor
query_sql: SELECT m.LIFNR, COUNT(*) AS total_gr, SUM(CASE WHEN m.MENGE = p.MENGE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS exact_matches, ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN m.MENGE = p.MENGE THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) * 100, 2) AS match_rate FROM main_staging.stg_sap__mseg m JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo p ON m.EBELN = p.EBELN AND m.EBELP = p.EBELP JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__mkpf mk ON m.MBLNR = mk.MBLNR AND m.MJAHR = mk.MJAHR WHERE m.BWART = '101' AND mk.BUDAT >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY m.LIFNR HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5 ORDER BY match_rate DESC LIMIT 10
result_summary: 8 rows. Shows actual accuracy rates by vendor to validate metric feasibility and expected ranges

query_description: Check for custom tolerance logic impact on accuracy calculation
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_gr, SUM(CASE WHEN m.MENGE > p.MENGE * 1.05 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS over_tolerance, SUM(CASE WHEN m.MENGE < p.MENGE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS under_delivery, SUM(CASE WHEN m.MENGE = p.MENGE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS exact_match FROM main_staging.stg_sap__mseg m JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo p ON m.EBELN = p.EBELN AND m.EBELP = p.EBELP WHERE m.BWART = '101'
result_summary: 1 rows. Assesses impact of 5% tolerance custom code - over-tolerance receipts should be rare due to ZMM_GR_QUANTITY_TOLERANCE

query_description: Analyze partial delivery patterns and multiple GR postings per PO line
query_sql: SELECT e.EBELN, e.EBELP, e.MENGE as po_qty, COUNT(*) as gr_count, SUM(m.MENGE) as total_received, CASE WHEN SUM(m.MENGE) = e.MENGE THEN 'exact_match' WHEN SUM(m.MENGE) < e.MENGE THEN 'partial' WHEN SUM(m.MENGE) > e.MENGE THEN 'over_delivery' END as delivery_type FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo e JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__mseg m ON e.EBELN = m.EBELN AND e.EBELP = m.EBELP WHERE m.BWART = '101' GROUP BY e.EBELN, e.EBELP, e.MENGE HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY gr_count DESC LIMIT 15
result_summary: 0 rows. Shows how multiple GR postings accumulate against single PO line items, critical for accurate match calculation
data_quality_notes: Need to handle cumulative receipts vs individual posting matches

query_description: Check for goods receipts that exceed 5% tolerance despite custom validation
query_sql: SELECT k.LIFNR, COUNT(*) as over_tolerance_count, COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() as pct_of_total FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo e JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__mseg m ON e.EBELN = m.EBELN AND e.EBELP = m.EBELP JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekko k ON e.EBELN = k.EBELN WHERE m.BWART = '101' AND m.MENGE > e.MENGE * 1.05 GROUP BY k.LIFNR ORDER BY over_tolerance_count DESC
result_summary: 0 rows. Confirms custom tolerance validation effectiveness - should show minimal over-tolerance cases
data_quality_notes: Any significant over-tolerance cases indicate validation bypass or data quality issues

query_description: Validate quarterly aggregation logic with sample vendor accuracy rates
query_sql: WITH quarterly_accuracy AS (SELECT k.LIFNR, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM p.BUDAT) as year, EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM p.BUDAT) as quarter, COUNT(*) as total_receipts, SUM(CASE WHEN m.MENGE = e.MENGE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as exact_matches, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN m.MENGE = e.MENGE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) as accuracy_pct FROM main_staging.stg_sap__mseg m JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__mkpf p ON m.MBLNR = p.MBLNR AND m.MJAHR = p.MJAHR JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo e ON m.EBELN = e.EBELN AND m.EBELP = e.EBELP JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekko k ON e.EBELN = k.EBELN WHERE m.BWART = '101' AND p.BUDAT >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY k.LIFNR, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM p.BUDAT), EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM p.BUDAT)) SELECT * FROM quarterly_accuracy WHERE total_receipts >= 10 ORDER BY accuracy_pct ASC LIMIT 10
result_summary: 10 rows. Validates complete quarterly calculation logic and identifies vendors with lowest accuracy for data quality review
data_quality_notes: Filters for minimum volume to ensure statistical significance

query_description: Profile EKPO line item values and currency distribution
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) as total_lines, COUNT(DISTINCT EBELN) as po_count, COUNT(NETWR) as netwr_populated, MIN(NETWR) as min_value, MAX(NETWR) as max_value, AVG(NETWR) as avg_value FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo WHERE NETWR IS NOT NULL
result_summary: 1 rows. Confirms NETWR completeness and value distribution across PO line items

query_description: Check currency usage and identify non-EUR values
query_sql: SELECT 'Currency column not available' as message, COUNT(*) as line_count, SUM(NETWR) as total_value FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo WHERE NETWR IS NOT NULL
result_summary: 1 rows. Validates if all values are in EUR or if currency conversion is needed

query_description: Join EKKO and EKPO to validate PO header-line relationship for period aggregation
query_sql: SELECT h.BEDAT, COUNT(DISTINCT h.EBELN) as po_count, COUNT(l.EBELP) as line_count, SUM(l.NETWR) as total_value FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko h INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo l ON h.EBELN = l.EBELN WHERE l.NETWR IS NOT NULL GROUP BY h.BEDAT ORDER BY h.BEDAT LIMIT 10
result_summary: 10 rows. Confirms clean header-line joins and daily aggregation feasibility

query_description: Sample high-value PO lines to validate data reasonableness
query_sql: SELECT h.EBELN, h.BEDAT, l.EBELP, l.MATNR, l.TXZ01, l.MENGE, l.NETWR FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko h INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo l ON h.EBELN = l.EBELN WHERE l.NETWR > 50000 ORDER BY l.NETWR DESC LIMIT 5
result_summary: 5 rows. Spot-check high-value lines for data quality and business logic validation

query_description: Test period-based aggregation for metric calculation
query_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', h.BEDAT) as period_month, COUNT(DISTINCT h.EBELN) as po_count, COUNT(l.EBELP) as line_count, SUM(l.NETWR) as total_po_value FROM main_staging.stg_sap__ekko h INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo l ON h.EBELN = l.EBELN WHERE l.NETWR IS NOT NULL GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', h.BEDAT) ORDER BY period_month LIMIT 6
result_summary: 6 rows. Validates monthly aggregation logic and confirms consistent data across periods

query_description: Identify CPE materials and their characteristics
query_sql: SELECT m.MTART, m.MATKL, t.WGBEZ, COUNT(DISTINCT m.MATNR) AS material_count, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN mc.SERNP IS NOT NULL THEN m.MATNR END) AS serialized_count FROM main_staging.stg_sap__mara m LEFT JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__t023 t ON m.MATKL = t.MATKL LEFT JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__marc mc ON m.MATNR = mc.MATNR WHERE m.MATKL LIKE '%CPE%' OR t.WGBEZ LIKE '%CPE%' OR m.MTART IN ('ZCPE', 'FERT') GROUP BY m.MTART, m.MATKL, t.WGBEZ ORDER BY material_count DESC
result_summary: 5 rows. Identifies which material types and groups contain CPE devices and confirms serial number profile setup
data_quality_notes: Need to validate CPE identification logic since it's not explicitly coded

query_description: Profile equipment master data for CPE devices
query_sql: SELECT e.EQART, COUNT(DISTINCT e.EQUNR) AS equipment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.SERGE) AS serial_count, MIN(e.INBDT) AS first_install, MAX(e.INBDT) AS last_install, COUNT(DISTINCT e.STAT_TEXT) AS status_variants FROM main_staging.stg_sap__equi e WHERE e.EQART = 'CPE' OR e.TYPBZ LIKE '%CPE%' GROUP BY e.EQART
result_summary: 1 rows. Confirms equipment master setup for CPE tracking and current status distribution
data_quality_notes: SERGE should match MSEG.SERNP for proper serial tracking

query_description: Analyze goods movement patterns for CPE lifecycle tracking
query_sql: SELECT m.BWART, t.BTEXT, COUNT(*) AS movement_count, COUNT(DISTINCT m.MATNR) AS materials, COUNT(DISTINCT m.SERNP) AS serial_numbers FROM main_staging.stg_sap__mseg m LEFT JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__t156 t ON m.BWART = t.BWART INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__mara ma ON m.MATNR = ma.MATNR WHERE m.BWART IN ('101', '201', '161', '122') AND (ma.MATKL LIKE '%CPE%' OR ma.MTART = 'ZCPE') AND m.SERNP IS NOT NULL GROUP BY m.BWART, t.BTEXT ORDER BY m.BWART
result_summary: 4 rows. Validates the movement types used for CPE lifecycle (101=GR, 201=deploy, 161=return, 122=defective) and confirms serial number tracking
data_quality_notes: Serial number SERNP should be populated for all CPE movements per custom code

query_description: Check serial number data quality and linking
query_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT m.SERNP) AS mseg_serials, COUNT(DISTINCT e.SERGE) AS equi_serials, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN e.SERGE IS NOT NULL THEN m.SERNP END) AS linked_serials, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN m.SERNP IS NOT NULL AND e.SERGE IS NULL THEN m.SERNP END) AS orphan_mseg_serials FROM main_staging.stg_sap__mseg m INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__mara ma ON m.MATNR = ma.MATNR LEFT JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__equi e ON m.SERNP = e.SERGE WHERE (ma.MATKL LIKE '%CPE%' OR ma.MTART = 'ZCPE') AND m.SERNP IS NOT NULL
result_summary: 1 rows. Validates that serial numbers in goods movements properly link to equipment master records
data_quality_notes: Custom code should create EQUI records automatically, so orphan serials indicate data quality issues

query_description: Sample current CPE deployment status logic
query_sql: WITH latest_movement AS (SELECT m.SERNP, m.MATNR, m.BWART, mk.BUDAT, mk.BUDAT as header_date, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY m.SERNP ORDER BY mk.BUDAT DESC, m.MBLNR DESC, m.ZEILE DESC) as rn FROM main_staging.stg_sap__mseg m INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__mkpf mk ON m.MBLNR = mk.MBLNR AND m.MJAHR = mk.MJAHR INNER JOIN main_staging.stg_sap__mara ma ON m.MATNR = ma.MATNR WHERE (ma.MATKL LIKE '%CPE%' OR ma.MTART = 'ZCPE') AND m.SERNP IS NOT NULL AND m.BWART IN ('101', '201', '161', '122')) SELECT lm.BWART, COUNT(*) as device_count, MIN(lm.header_date) as earliest_date, MAX(lm.header_date) as latest_date FROM latest_movement lm WHERE lm.rn = 1 GROUP BY lm.BWART ORDER BY lm.BWART
result_summary: 1 rows. Shows current status distribution of CPE devices based on latest movement type (201=deployed/active should be the target population)
data_quality_notes: Movement type 201 represents deployed devices, which should be the numerator for Active Deployed CPE Count
